title: Latin Cognates - AL Endings
description: Words ending in -AL convert directly to Spanish with stress on final syllable. Introduction to 'es' (is/it is).

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: English and Spanish are two related languages. English is considered a Germanic language but it's kind of half Latin. We have a huge amount of Latin vocabulary in English and this Latin vocabulary we can convert over to Spanish really easily because Spanish is practically modern Latin.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: So we can identify Latin words in English and we can with just some rule or other convert them over to Spanish and this will give us around 3,000 words.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: One of these rules is that words ending in A-L tend to come from Latin so we can make them Spanish. For example, normal in Spanish is normal, but we stress the end: normal.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: So if we assume the same happens with other words, how do you think you would say metal?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Metal. Good. To get a perfect accent in Spanish we just need to pronounce all of the vowels exactly as they are written. A, E, I, O, U in Spanish is ah, eh, ee, oh, oo.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: The word for 'is' in Spanish is es. E-S. And this also means 'it is', 'she is', 'he is', or even 'you are' when talking to somebody formally.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: So now we can say 'he is normal' or 'she is normal'. How would you say that?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es normal. And you pronounce the S, which is excellent. English speakers should be aware that maybe they feel themselves pronouncing a Z because many times we have an S in English like in the word 'is' and we pronounce a Z. Be careful to pronounce an S.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say legal?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Legal. Good. So how would you say 'it is legal'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es legal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say illegal?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Illegal. Good. In Spanish the I is like 'ink' and the E is like 'elephant'. Illegal.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: So how would you say 'it is illegal'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es illegal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: What if you want to say 'he is liberal'? You do not need the word for 'he' if you know who you are talking about. You can just use es. How would you say it?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es liberal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Now Spanish is much more phonetic than English. The letters represent the same sound. So with 'natural', you don't just want to change the accent and say it like English. You want to think how it's written and pronounce the letters as they are.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How do you think you would say natural in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Natural. Very good. You pronounce a T, A is like ah, U is like oo. Natural. And the accent on the end as well.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: If you want to say 'it isn't' or 'it's not', the word for 'not' or 'don't' is the same as the word for 'no'. And the word for 'no' in Spanish is the same as English, but you pronounce it like Spanish: no.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: You put this before 'es' if you want to say 'it is not'. How would that sound? How would you say 'it is not'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No es. Good. So now we can say things like 'it's not normal'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'it's not normal'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No es normal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say ideal? Really think about how it's spelled and try to pronounce each of those vowels as they are written. I like ink, E like elephant, A like apple.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ideal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'it's ideal'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es ideal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'it's not ideal'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No es ideal. Perfect.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say fatal?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Fatal. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say colonial?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Colonial. Good.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say cultural? Think about how it's written.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Cultural. Yes, and perfect Spanish accent. Cultural. Very nice.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say annual? And cut out that 'y' sound that comes in English. Focus on how it's written.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Anual. Very nice.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say dental?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Dental. Good. Tooth in Spanish is diente. So this is coming from dental. If you look up tooth in the dictionary and find diente, rather than treating it as random vocabulary to memorize, you can relate it back to dental.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say verbal?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Verbal. Good. So if we wanted to say 'verb', we can guess that verb is probably going to be similar in Spanish.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How do you think you say verb in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Verbo. Good. With vocabulary, we can happily guess and try. If we said 'verb' and they look at us funny and say 'ah, verbo!', then we learn. It's the best way to learn quickly.
